plan_adherence_notes: All 3 plan steps executed faithfully. Step 1: 9/9 AssumptionContext tests match the plan exactly (initial state, add, dedup, remove, clearAll, has, get, contextPayload, throw-without-provider). Step 2: 8/8 planned SessionContext tests present plus a bonus isConfigured-false test (9 total). Step 3: all 18 tests pass. All 4 decisions (d1-d4) followed: files created from scratch, renderHook with inline wrapper, placed in __tests__/, localStorage manipulated directly. Minor deduction: file sizes (83 and 71 lines) closely match plan targets (~90 and ~70). Test count is 18 vs the 17 planned (extra isConfigured false case is a good addition).
missed_questions: Should tests verify behavior with multiple simultaneous consumers of the same provider?
Should tests verify that localStorage.setItem errors are handled gracefully (e.g., quota exceeded)?
Should tests verify re-render behavior when state changes (e.g., component using the context re-renders with new values)?
reason: Implementation is complete and correct. Both test files cover the full API surface of their respective contexts with clean, idiomatic renderHook/act patterns. All 18 tests pass. The research phase did excellent work correcting the question phase's wrong assumptions (nonexistent test files, nonexistent setAllAssumptions function). Test quality is high: proper setup/teardown with beforeEach, good use of typed test fixtures, and both happy paths and error boundaries tested. The only gaps are minor edge cases (multiple consumers, localStorage quota errors) that are reasonable to omit at this scope.
